title: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system
content: Crypto usage has spiked in the country, with transaction volumes hitting $430 million in the year after the central bank removed restrictions in mid-2024.
summary: Bolivia is considering integrating Tether's USDT into its national payments system. This initiative follows a significant surge in crypto usage within the country, with transaction volumes reaching $430 million in the year after the central bank lifted restrictions in mid-2024.

title: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce
content: The 54 firm-strong group, which is backed by the City of London Corporation, will spend the next year working on live tokenisation use cases across UK financial markets.
summary: Major financial institutions like BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley have joined a UK government-backed taskforce. This 54-firm group will spend the next year developing and implementing live tokenization use cases across the UK's financial markets.

title: Solo bitcoin miner makes $200,000 using $150 equipment
content: Solo Bitcoin mining has surged, with 24 blocks found in the past 12 months, a 41% increase year over year.
summary: A solo Bitcoin miner recently achieved a significant payout of $200,000 using only $150 worth of equipment. This event comes amidst a notable surge in solo Bitcoin mining, which has seen a 41% year-over-year increase, resulting in 24 blocks being found by solo miners in the last 12 months.

title: Bitcoin slips as traders lift July Fed rate hike bets ahead of Inflation report
content: Major cryptocurrencies have dropped by 2% or more in 24 hours as traders boosted bets of a July Fed rate hike.
summary: Bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies have dropped in value as traders anticipate a potential July Fed rate hike, with many cryptocurrencies losing 2% or more in 24 hours. This shift is likely due to increased bets on a rate hike ahead of an upcoming inflation report. The cryptocurrency market is reacting to expected changes in monetary policy.

title: Binance.US CEO says exchange is rebuilding, eyes return to 20% U.S. market share
content: Binance.US is betting ultra-low fees, new regulated products and deeper liquidity will help it regain customers after two years of regulatory setbacks.
summary: Binance.US aims to recover from two years of regulatory challenges and regain its 20% U.S. market share. The exchange plans to achieve this through ultra-low fees, introducing new regulated products, and fostering deeper liquidity to attract customers.

title: Franklin Crypto CIO says crypto prices are disconnected from fundamentals
content: Institutional adoption of crypto is accelerating even as digital asset prices fail to reflect the industry's strongest fundamentals in years, Seth Ginns said.
summary: Franklin Crypto CIO Seth Ginns states that institutional adoption of crypto is accelerating despite digital asset prices not reflecting the industry's strong fundamentals. He believes the prices are disconnected from the actual state of the industry. This discrepancy suggests a potential for growth or correction in the market.

title: TeraWulf CEO: 'Not All Megawatts Are Created Equally' in AI Race
content: TeraWulf says its $19 billion AI hosting agreement with Anthropic underscores its transformation from a Bitcoin miner into an AI infrastructure company.
summary: TeraWulf, a former Bitcoin miner, has signed a $19 billion AI hosting agreement with Anthropic, marking its transformation into an AI infrastructure company. This deal underscores the company's shift in focus from cryptocurrency to artificial intelligence. The agreement highlights TeraWulf's efforts to become a major player in the AI industry.

title: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
content: Discussion among Democrats have fixed on the president's personal gains when hashing out the crypto market structure bill's ethics provision.
summary: Discussions among Democrats regarding the proposed "Clarity Act" crypto market structure bill are being significantly influenced by former President Trump's personal cryptocurrency holdings. These holdings are central to the talks concerning the bill's ethics provision, which aims to ban conflicts of interest for U.S. officials.

title: Mizuho says Circle bank approval doesn't solve USDC growth, stablecoin competition risks
content: Japanese investment bank Mizuho reiterated its neutral rating on Circle, saying OCC approval for a national trust bank doesn't address slowing USDC growth or rising competition.
summary: Japanese investment bank Mizuho maintained a neutral rating on Circle, stating that the OCC's approval for a national trust bank does not address slowing growth for its USDC stablecoin. Mizuho also highlighted ongoing risks posed by increasing competition within the stablecoin market.

title: Strategy pauses its Bitcoin buying spree to hoard a massive $3 billion cash cushion
content: Strategy now has more than 20 months of coverage for preferred-stock dividends and debt interest.
summary: MicroStrategy (Strategy) has paused its aggressive Bitcoin buying strategy, instead opting to accumulate a substantial $3 billion cash reserve. This significant cash cushion provides the company with over 20 months of coverage for its preferred-stock dividends and debt interest.

title: Robinhood built a blockchain for tokenized stocks. Memecoins took over
content: Robinhood Chain has pulled in $135 million of value and 800,000 addresses since July 1. Almost none of it is doing what the chain was built for.
summary: Robinhood launched its own blockchain, 'Robinhood Chain,' with the intention of facilitating tokenized stocks. However, since its July 1 launch, the chain has accumulated $135 million in value and 800,000 addresses, primarily being utilized for memecoins rather than its original purpose.

title: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model
content: A Treasury-backed report targets putting repo, gilts and funds onchain within two years, and warms to permissionless networks that firms like Ripple have been building on.
summary: A UK Treasury-backed report outlines plans to tokenize financial instruments like repo, gilts, and funds, aiming for on-chain implementation within two years. The report expresses support for permissionless networks, referencing firms like Ripple as examples of companies utilizing this technology.

title: Robinhood Chain surges into top five by DEX volume: Bernstein
content: Robinhood's new blockchain quickly became a top-five network by DEX volume, signaling strong early demand for its tokenized asset platform, the broker said.
summary: Robinhood's new blockchain has quickly ascended to become one of the top five networks by decentralized exchange (DEX) volume. This rapid growth suggests strong early demand for its tokenized asset platform.

title: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative pivots to Solana for tokenization, stablecoin issuance
content: The SBI Solana Global joint venture now includes the Solana Foundation, the Swiss organization that oversees the layer-1 network.
summary: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative is pivoting to Solana for tokenization and stablecoin issuance. Their SBI Solana Global joint venture has also expanded to include the Solana Foundation.

title: Resurgent U.S.-Iran hostilities send bitcoin lower even as ETF flows show demand
content: Your day-ahead look for July 13, 2026
summary: Bitcoin prices saw a downturn following renewed hostilities between the U.S. and Iran, despite strong underlying demand indicated by ETF flows. This report provides a forward-looking market analysis for July 13, 2026.

title: Paradigm shifts vs bubbles: AI chips and bitcoin show powerful trends can still produce severe corrections
content: Structural change can create lasting opportunities, but explosive rallies in semiconductors, metals and bitcoin show how quickly strong narratives can become speculative excess.
summary: The article discusses how powerful structural trends, exemplified by AI chips and Bitcoin, can create lasting opportunities but are also prone to severe market corrections. It highlights the risk of strong narratives quickly turning into speculative excess, leading to explosive rallies and subsequent deflations.

title: US government moves $297M in seized Bitcoin, Ether to Coinbase Prime
content: The transfers have raised questions over Trump’s Bitcoin reserve pledge, though deposits to Coinbase Prime do not confirm an impending sale.
summary: The US government has transferred $297 million in seized Bitcoin and Ether to Coinbase Prime, sparking speculation about a potential sale, although deposits do not necessarily confirm this. The move has also raised questions about Trump's Bitcoin reserve pledge. The transfer is a significant development in the crypto space.

title: Global law firm launches MiCA compliance tool as crypto companies navigate new EU rules
content: Reed Smith’s Aquarius platform automates regulatory filings and legal workflows as demand for MiCA compliance tools grows across Europe.
summary: Global law firm Reed Smith has launched a compliance tool called Aquarius to help crypto companies navigate new EU rules under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. The tool automates regulatory filings and legal workflows. This launch comes as demand for MiCA compliance tools grows across Europe.

title: Bolivia mulls recognizing USDT as payment currency amid dollar shortage
content: As foreign currency reserves remain under pressure, Bolivia is considering a framework to let the world’s largest stablecoin be used for payments, savings and trade.
summary: Bolivia is exploring a framework to officially recognize USDT, the world's largest stablecoin, for payments, savings, and trade. This consideration comes as the country faces ongoing pressure on its foreign currency reserves and aims to address a dollar shortage.

title: Hyundai completes USDT treasury settlement pilot between US and Mexico
content: Hyundai completed a proof-of-concept using Tether's USDT to settle a cross-border treasury transfer between its US and Mexican subsidiaries, highlighting growing enterprise interest in stablecoin-based payments.
summary: Hyundai successfully piloted a cross-border treasury settlement between its US and Mexican subsidiaries using Tether's USDT stablecoin. This proof-of-concept demonstrates increasing enterprise interest in leveraging stablecoins for international payments.

title: Donald Trump invokes US senator’s death to push crypto bill
content: With the death of Senator Lindsey Graham and another senator hospitalized, Republicans’ current majority in the chamber has been reduced to 51-47, likely requiring more Democratic support to pass crypto market structure.
summary: US Senator Lindsey Graham's death has reduced the Republican majority in the chamber, potentially affecting the passage of a crypto market structure bill. The new balance of power may require more Democratic support for the bill to pass. This development could impact the shape of cryptocurrency regulation in the US.

title: Coinbase Ventures tops crypto VC list for H1 2026
content: Coinbase’s venture capital arm leads 2026 crypto investing even as funding withered and unique investors shied away amid the bear market.
summary: Coinbase Ventures has been identified as the leading venture capital investor in the crypto sector for the first half of 2026. This achievement occurred despite a challenging bear market, which saw overall funding decline and fewer unique investors engaging in the space.

title: UK tokenization push could add as much as $44B to annual output by 2035: Report
content: The government-backed roadmap calls for the UK’s first digital gilt by early 2027 and seeks to make tokenized bonds usable for trading and borrowing.
summary: The UK government has announced a plan to tokenize assets, aiming to release its first digital gilt by 2027, which could increase annual output by $44B by 2035. This move is expected to make tokenized bonds usable for trading and borrowing. The plan is part of a broader effort to modernize the UK's financial system.

title: Bitcoin whale moves $188M for first time in 7 years
content: A dormant whale transferred BTC worth $188 million after seven years of holding, adding to the growing ratio of whale transfers to cryptocurrency exchanges.
summary: A dormant Bitcoin whale has transferred $188 million worth of BTC for the first time in 7 years, contributing to the increasing trend of whale transfers to cryptocurrency exchanges. This move may indicate a significant shift in market dynamics. The transfer's impact on the market remains to be seen.

title: Binance June futures volume at $1.6T defies crypto spot trading slump
content: Binance futures volume jumped 80% in June, to $1.61 trillion, outpacing rivals while broader crypto trading activity remained weak.
summary: Binance futures volume increased by 80% in June to $1.61 trillion, despite a slump in broader crypto spot trading activity. This growth outpaced its rivals, showcasing a strong performance for Binance. The surge in futures volume indicates a significant trend in the crypto market.

title: BTC price bull market to begin in September? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
content: Bitcoin price analysis called for the end of the bear market within three months as the US-Iran war sent BTC lower from strengthening $64,000 resistance.
summary: Bitcoin price analysis predicts the end of the bear market within three months, with a potential bull market starting in September. The US-Iran war caused a dip in BTC price after reaching $64,000 resistance. The analysis suggests a potential upside for Bitcoin in the coming months.

title: Japan stablecoin payments advance with Lawson trial, Netstars launch
content: Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo as Netstars launches a merchant service supporting USDC, USDT and JPYC.
summary: Japan's Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo, while Netstars is launching a merchant service that supports USDC, USDT, and JPYC. This move marks a significant advancement in stablecoin payments in the country. The trial and launch aim to increase the adoption of cryptocurrency in everyday transactions.

title: AI microbusinesses could drive $262B in stablecoin volume by 2033: Swyftx
content: The AI-native cohort of the expanding gig economy could increasingly use stablecoins to avoid slow and expensive traditional payment rails, Australian crypto exchange Swyftx said.
summary: Australian crypto exchange Swyftx predicts that AI microbusinesses could drive $262 billion in stablecoin volume by 2033. This growth is anticipated as the AI-native gig economy increasingly adopts stablecoins to bypass slow and expensive traditional payment systems.

title: Bitcoin ETFs draw $197M, snap 8-week outflow streak
content: Analysts are not yet ready to call it a recovery in institutional demand for Bitcoin.
summary: Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds attracted $197 million in inflows, breaking an eight-week streak of outflows. Despite this positive movement, analysts remain hesitant to declare a full recovery in institutional demand for Bitcoin.

title: Robinhood L2 sparks ETH optimism, Saylor 'muddies waters.' Hodler's Digest, July 5-12, 2026
content: Even Ethereum's critics say Robinhood Chain is bullish for ETH. Nigel Farage and US President Donald Trump become embroiled in crypto related scandals, and more.
summary: Robinhood Chain, an L2 solution, is sparking optimism for Ethereum, with even critics acknowledging its bullish impact on ETH. This development coincides with political figures Nigel Farage and Donald Trump becoming involved in crypto-related scandals.

title: Strategy's Saylor needs clarity in BTC pivot message to convince investors: StanChart
content: Standard Chartered sees communication challenges facing the biggest digital asset treasury company as "muddying the waters" for Bitcoin in the near term.
summary: Standard Chartered believes communication challenges from the largest digital asset treasury company, implied to be MicroStrategy and Michael Saylor, are creating near-term uncertainty for Bitcoin. The bank suggests Saylor needs to provide more clarity in his Bitcoin pivot message to reassure investors.

title: Empery Digital shares rise after selling BTC to fund AI data center project
content: The sales come months after a major Empery shareholder demanded the firm ditch its Bitcoin treasury strategy and sought the resignation of its CEO and board.
summary: Empery Digital's shares increased after the company sold its Bitcoin treasury, a move intended to fund an AI data center project. This strategic pivot follows a major shareholder's demand for the firm to abandon its Bitcoin strategy.

title: Bonzo Lend loses $9M in oracle exploit on Hedera
content: An attacker inflated the value of SAUCE collateral and borrowed $9 million from Bonzo Lend through a flaw in Supra’s onchain oracle verifier.
summary: Bonzo Lend suffered a $9 million loss due to an oracle exploit on the Hedera network. An attacker exploited a flaw in Supra's on-chain oracle verifier to inflate the value of SAUCE collateral and borrow funds.

title: How Bitcoin ETFs Changed Institutional Adoption
content: A spot bitcoin ETF is a fund that holds actual bitcoin and trades on a stock exchange, so an investor gains exposure to bitcoin’s price through an ordinary brokerage account. When the first U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs began trading in January 2024, they removed the custody, compliance, and operational barriers that had kept many asset […]
summary: The introduction of spot bitcoin ETFs in the US has removed barriers to investment, allowing institutions to gain exposure to bitcoin's price through ordinary brokerage accounts. This change has facilitated institutional adoption of bitcoin. The first US spot bitcoin ETFs began trading in January 2024.

title: UK lays out tokenized finance roadmap with projected £33 billion annual boost
content: Industry leaders say payment infrastructure must evolve alongside tokenized assets to enable real-time settlement.
summary: The UK has outlined a roadmap for tokenized finance, which is expected to bring a £33 billion annual boost. Industry leaders emphasize the need for payment infrastructure to evolve and support real-time settlement for tokenized assets. This development aims to enhance the country's financial system.

title: President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms
content: President Trump and other key administration officials and lawmakers are making a renewed push to pass cryptocurrency legislation into law.
summary: President Trump and administration officials are pushing for the passage of the Clarity Act, a cryptocurrency legislation, despite an impending ethics fight. The act aims to advance cryptocurrency regulation. This development may impact the crypto industry.

title: Japan’s largest security token platform moves nearly $3 billion to Avalanche blockchain
content: Progmat develops security token infrastructure that accounts for 64.6% of Japan’s security token market total issuance value.
summary: Progmat, Japan's largest security token platform, has moved nearly $3 billion to the Avalanche blockchain, holding 64.6% of Japan's security token market total issuance value. This move signifies a major shift in the country's security token infrastructure. The transition underscores the growing importance of blockchain technology in Japan's financial sector.

title: Tom Lee says users ‘starting to see Ethereum as money’ as Bitmine adds 27,801 ETH
content: Bitmine accumulates 5.77M ETH tokens, 4.8% of supply, as Tom Lee cites Robinhood Chain driving everyday user adoption.
summary: Tom Lee notes that users are starting to view Ethereum as a form of money, driven by the everyday adoption spurred by Robinhood Chain. Bitmine has accumulated 5.77M ETH tokens, representing 4.8% of the total supply, with a recent addition of 27,801 ETH. This growth indicates a significant interest in Ethereum.

title: Robinhood Chain draws over $3 billion in weekly DEX volume to join top five chains: Bernstein
content: Robinhood Chain hit $3.1B in weekly DEX volume and 65K users since its July 1 launch, as the Layer 2 eyes tokenized markets, Bernstein said.
summary: Robinhood Chain has reached $3.1 billion in weekly DEX volume and 65,000 users since its launch on July 1, according to Bernstein. The Layer 2 chain is eyeing tokenized markets. This milestone places Robinhood Chain among the top five chains.

title: Strategy sells $467 million in MSTR shares, makes no bitcoin purchases as USD reserve hits $3 billion
content: Strategy's total holdings remain at 843,775 BTC and account for around 4% of the 21 million bitcoin supply cap — worth roughly $53 billion.
summary: Strategy sold $467 million in MSTR shares and has a USD reserve of $3 billion, with no new bitcoin purchases, while holding 843,775 BTC worth $53 billion. The company's bitcoin holdings account for around 4% of the total bitcoin supply cap. This move indicates a significant shift in Strategy's asset management.

title: SBI Holdings, Solana Foundation partner to build Japan-based onchain financial market
content: SBI and the Solana Foundation are collaborating to build an onchain financial market with SBI R3 rebranding to SBI Solana Global.
summary: SBI Holdings is partnering with the Solana Foundation to build a Japan-based on-chain financial market, with SBI R3 being rebranded to SBI Solana Global. This collaboration aims to create a new financial market infrastructure. The partnership marks a significant development in the adoption of blockchain technology in Japan's financial sector.

title: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report
content: The central bank and the SEC have reportedly begun using data analytics tools to examine abnormal stablecoin trades, with a focus on USDT.
summary: The Bank of Thailand and the SEC are auditing high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance, using data analytics tools with a focus on USDT. This effort aims to regulate and monitor stablecoin transactions. The move is part of a broader initiative to prevent financial crimes.

title: Japan’s Lawson convenience store pilots stablecoin payments with JPYC: report
content: This is part of a proof-of-concept involving telecommunications giant KDDI and crypto wallet firm HashPort.
summary: Japan's Lawson convenience store chain is piloting stablecoin payments using JPYC in a proof-of-concept. This initiative involves collaborations with telecommunications giant KDDI and crypto wallet firm HashPort.

title: The Funding: Why Japan’s SBI Holdings is investing big in crypto
content: The Japanese financial giant's recent crypto deals offer insights into how traditional finance is approaching digital assets.
summary: Japan's financial giant, SBI Holdings, is significantly investing in the crypto space. These recent deals offer valuable insights into how traditional finance institutions are evaluating and engaging with digital assets.

title: Bitwise Sees a Bottom in Bitcoin’s Worst Vibes Yet: ‘Darkest Before the Dawn’
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitwise Sees a Bottom in Bitcoin’s Worst Vibes Yet: ‘Darkest Before the Dawn’ 
Bitwise says Bitcoin's brutal nine-month downturn masks a stronger-than-ever foundation, arguing institutional adoption, corporate buying, and improving market infrastructure position the industry for a rebound despite record ETF outflows and weak sentiment.
This post Bitwise Sees a Bottom in Bitcoin’s Worst Vibes Yet: ‘Darkest Before the Dawn’  first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitwise believes that despite Bitcoin's 9-month downturn, the foundation for the industry is stronger than ever due to institutional adoption and improving market infrastructure, positioning it for a rebound. The company argues that the current weak sentiment masks a positive outlook. Record ETF outflows have occurred during this time.

title: ‘Don’t Let China Win’: President Trump Presses Senate on Clarity Act in Final Stretch
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

‘Don’t Let China Win’: President Trump Presses Senate on Clarity Act in Final Stretch
President Trump urged the Senate to pass the CLARITY Act before the August recess as lawmakers face a narrowing window and unresolved political hurdles.
This post ‘Don’t Let China Win’: President Trump Presses Senate on Clarity Act in Final Stretch first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: President Trump is pressing the Senate to pass the CLARITY Act before the August recess, citing the need to not let China win, as lawmakers face a narrowing window and unresolved political hurdles. The act is related to cryptocurrency regulation. The Senate is facing a time-sensitive decision on the matter.

title: Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched
Strategy (MSTR) raised $467 million through stock sales to boost its cash reserve to $3 billion while leaving its 843,775 BTC holdings unchanged for a second straight week.
This post Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strategy (MSTR) has raised $467 million in cash through stock sales, increasing its cash reserve to $3 billion, while keeping its large holding of 843,775 Bitcoin unchanged. This move indicates the company's confidence in its Bitcoin holdings. The funds will likely be used to further the company's business operations.

title: U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
Rep. French Hill urged the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act before the August recess, arguing the bill would establish a transparent crypto market framework that addresses ethics concerns surrounding President Trump's digital asset ventures.
This post U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: US Representatives are urging the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act in July, which aims to establish a transparent crypto market framework and address ethics concerns. The bill is seen as a way to provide clarity on digital asset regulation. Rep. French Hill argues that passing the bill before the August recess is crucial.

title: Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
Circle won final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, allowing it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expanding its regulated digital asset custody business.
This post Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Circle has received final approval from the OCC to establish a national trust bank, enabling it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expand its digital asset custody business. This move allows for greater regulatory oversight and stability. Circle's national trust bank will operate under federal supervision.

title: Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan
Metaplanet is taking its Bitcoin strategy beyond treasury accumulation, launching a study to explore tokenized, Bitcoin-backed credit products that could reshape Japan's debt market.
This post Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Metaplanet is launching a joint study to introduce Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan, aiming to transform the country's debt market. This initiative goes beyond Metaplanet's existing Bitcoin accumulation strategy. The study will explore tokenized credit products backed by Bitcoin.

title: Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
Standard Chartered reaffirmed its $100,000 end-2026 Bitcoin price target, calling Bitcoin "a screaming buy" and arguing that Strategy's recent sell-off reflects a communication issue around its new monetization strategy rather than a weakening Bitcoin outlook.
This post Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Standard Chartered has reaffirmed its $100,000 Bitcoin price target by the end of 2026, calling it a 'screaming buy'. The company attributes Strategy's recent sell-off to a communication issue, not a weakening Bitcoin outlook. This suggests a positive outlook for Bitcoin's future value.

title: JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
JPMorgan says Strategy's Bitcoin sales are a short-term concern, but the bigger long-term risk is that banks and institutions adopt private blockchains over public networks, reducing activity and capital flows across the broader crypto ecosystem.
This post JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: JPMorgan believes the real long-term threat to Bitcoin is not Strategy's Bitcoin sales, but rather the adoption of private blockchains by banks and institutions, which could reduce activity and capital flows in the crypto ecosystem. This could have a significant impact on the broader crypto market. The institution views this as a bigger risk than short-term sales.

title: Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
BitcoinTreasuries.net says June marked the first major stress test for Bitcoin-backed preferred shares, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the growing corporate Bitcoin financing model.
This post Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin-backed preferred shares faced their first major stress test in June, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the corporate Bitcoin financing model. This test is significant for the growing Bitcoin financing sector. The outcome has reinforced confidence in the model.

content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. The company's latest discovery has garnered attention, but its implications are still being debated. Anthropic's research pushes the boundaries of AI capabilities and understanding.

This discovery sheds light on how these models think and understand information. The finding is significant for the development of AI technology.

What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…
summary: Anthropic developed a technique to better understand large language models, finding a range of results from mundane to unnerving. The tool, called the Jacobian lens, provides insight into the models' thought processes. This discovery could significantly impact AI development.

title: EmTech AI 2026: The Rise of the AI Platform
summary: The EmTech AI 2026 conference discusses the growing importance of AI platforms, highlighting their potential to transform industries and revolutionize the way we live and work. The conference explores the latest advancements in AI technology and its applications. AI platforms are expected to play a crucial role in shaping the future of various sectors.

content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI Sam Altman’s proposal that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI is back in the spotlight, with reports that he is discussing giving…
summary: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman proposes that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI, with a potential $300 stake for families. This idea is being discussed, sparking interest in the tech community. The Treasury also issued an AI warning, highlighting the significance of AI's impact.

title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
content: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.
summary: The last wave of tech winners are working hard again, driven by the fear of missing out on AI's defining moment and the potential to make more money. They are rolling up their sleeves to capitalize on the opportunities presented by AI. This move is likely motivated by the prospect of significant financial gains.

title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
content: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.
summary: PixVerse, a video-generation startup, has raised $439M, with its valuation now exceeding $2B. The company plans to use the funds to expand its world model offering and reach a broader customer base globally. This investment underscores the growing interest in AI-powered video generation.

title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
content: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.
summary: Satya Nadella has warned companies about the potential risks of using AI, specifically the concern that AI labs selling proprietary models may be acting like Trojan horses. This warning highlights the potential downsides of AI and the need for caution. The warning is significant for the AI community.

title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
content: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.
summary: Apple's trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI includes allegations of unauthorized access to Apple's systems and claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. The lawsuit contains various eye-catching claims, including employees joking about accessing Apple's systems. The allegations suggest a significant breach of trust between the two companies.

title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
content: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?
summary: The article explores a hypothetical scenario where AI is used to assist in illegal activities, such as getting away with killing a spouse, raising questions about the ethics of user-aligned AI. It delves into the potential consequences of creating AI systems that prioritize user goals above all else. The article prompts consideration of the moral implications of AI development.

title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
content: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that the company stole trade secrets, with senior leadership, including a former Apple employee, involved in the misconduct. The lawsuit claims that OpenAI's actions were intentional and directed by its top officials. This case has significant implications for the tech industry.

title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
content: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]
summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue states that open source AI is booming, with the company becoming a hub for AI builders to share and download models and datasets, now used by half of the Fortune 500. This growth indicates a significant shift in the AI industry. Hugging Face has become a key player in the open source AI space.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
content: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.
summary: SK Hynix has raised $26.5B in the largest foreign IPO in US history, driven by the AI chip boom. The company, along with Samsung, is being urged to build new factories in the US. This development highlights the growing importance of AI chips and the need for domestic manufacturing.

title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
content: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]
summary: Hugging Face's CEO, Clem Delangue, states that open source AI is booming, with the company serving as a platform for AI builders to share and download models and datasets, used by half of the Fortune 500. Companies are shifting away from renting AI and towards open source solutions. This trend indicates a significant change in the AI industry.

title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.
summary: OpenAI's new models, including GPT 5.6, will continue to power Microsoft's workplace and productivity apps, amid speculation of a potential breakup between the two companies. This ensures continuity for Microsoft's Copilot 365. The partnership remains intact for now.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
content: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's No. 2 executive, is stepping down from her role after a longer-than-expected medical leave, creating a leadership vacuum at a crucial time for the company. OpenAI is considering an IPO and competing with Anthropic in the enterprise market. This change may impact OpenAI's strategic direction and growth plans.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
content: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.
summary: OpenAI has launched a new family of models, GPT-5.6, which promises improvements in various areas, including cybersecurity. The new models are expected to enhance performance and capabilities. This launch is a significant update from OpenAI.

title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
content: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.
summary: Lyzr, a startup building AI agents, used its own AI agent to raise $100 million, demonstrating the product's effectiveness. This achievement showcases the potential of AI in handling complex tasks, such as fundraising. The successful raise highlights the viability of AI agents in enterprise settings.

title: The 6 wildest claims in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI
content:
When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product samples. That's according to a blockbuster lawsuit filed by Apple, which accuses OpenAI of stealing confidential documents, spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking one of its trusted partners into performing a proprietary product design technique.
The lawsuit primarily revolves around the alleged actions of three people:
Tang Tan, a 24-year Apple veteran who recently served as the vice president of the Apple Watch. In 2024, Tan left to work on …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, accusing the startup of stealing confidential documents, spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking a partner into performing a proprietary product design technique. The lawsuit centers around the actions of three individuals, including a former Apple vice president. The allegations include requests for Apple employees to bring unreleased product samples to OpenAI.

title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
content:
Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple's on-device AI processing. 
The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering Face ID, Animoji, and  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple's failed self-driving car program unexpectedly led to the development of the company's powerful on-device AI chips. The necessity for robust AI processing for the car project resulted in the creation of the Neural Engine. This crucial technology, first seen in the A11 Bionic and iPhone X, now underpins Apple's AI capabilities for features like FaceID.

title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
content:
A yard sign opposing a planned data center is displayed along Route 54 in Mount Carmel Township Northumberland County. | Image: Getty Images	

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
Years before the AI boom threatened local power grids, a small group of protesters set the stage for the battles cropping up across communities today. In 2015, Apple announced plans to build a roughly $1 billion data center in the sleepy town of Athenry, Ireland. The data center's 500-acre site would power Apple's services in Europe, including iTunes, iMessage, and Siri. At th …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The article highlights the increasing opposition to data centers, particularly those supporting the AI boom, due to concerns about their impact on local power grids and communities. It traces these disputes from early protests against an Apple data center in Ireland in 2015 to current battles in various locations.

title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
content:
Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI creations without the account owner's permission.
"Earlier this week, we announced that one way for people to generate images in Meta AI is by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they want to reference," Meta says in an update to a blog post about its new Muse Image AI model. "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give pe …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Meta is shutting down an Instagram feature that allowed users to create AI deepfakes of public accounts without permission, following significant backlash. The feature was intended to be a creative tool but raised concerns about user consent. It has been discontinued just days after its announcement.

title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
content:
Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Products - Jony Ive's hardware startup, which OpenAI bought in 2025 - along with two specific employees, Tang Tan (OpenAI's chief hardware officer) and Chang Liu (who joined OpenAI from Apple in January).
An Apple spokesperson shared this statement with 9to5Mac:
At Apple, our teams are constantly developing breakthrough technologies to create the b …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to hardware, claiming that former Apple employees now at OpenAI used this information to advance the startup's hardware plans. The lawsuit also names IO Products and two specific employees. This lawsuit could have significant implications for both Apple and OpenAI.

title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
content:
Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter out AI content," Mosseri said during an interview on Lenny Rachitsky's podcast. "I think we should let you know if content is AI content or not."
At the same time, Mosseri seems to be drawing a distinction between content-based sorting and banning AI from the platform entirely. In fact, he believes people who love AI content "should be able to have a feed that's just AI town." Instagram, like many other platforms, including TikTok, YouTube, and Fac …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri stated that users who don't like AI-generated content shouldn't have it in their feed, and instead, the platform should let users know if content is AI-generated or not. Mosseri believes in giving users the option to have an AI-only feed if they prefer. He draws a distinction between sorting and banning AI content entirely.

title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
content:
A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute nodes in homes equipped with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems." Customers will be "compensated" for participating in the pilot program. 
Sunrun plans to sell the distributed compute power from the nodes to "enterprise compute buyers," like AI companies. It's a new approach to finding resources and space for AI compute infrastructure, as da …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Sunrun, a solar and home energy storage company, is launching a pilot program to place AI compute units in customers' homes, compensating them for participation, and selling the distributed compute power to enterprise buyers. The program aims to provide a new approach to AI compute infrastructure. Customers with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems are eligible to participate.

title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
content: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that a former Apple engineer used a bug to steal trade secrets and conspired with OpenAI. The engineer allegedly used the stolen information to benefit OpenAI. The lawsuit claims a breach of contract and misappropriation of trade secrets.

title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
content: "Context bombing" tricks hacking agents into shutting down before they can do harm.
summary: Defenders are using a technique called 'context bombing' or 'prompt injection' to trick hacking agents into shutting down before they can cause harm. This method involves providing misleading context to the hacking agents. The technique is showing promise in preventing cyber attacks.

title: Simulating everything, sort of: The promise and limits of world models
content: Experts explain how they work, what they can do, and what's still unsettled.
summary: Experts discuss the capabilities and limitations of world models, which are simulating complex systems. These models can mimic various aspects of reality, but their potential and current state are still being explored. World models have significant implications for fields like artificial intelligence.

summary: OpenAI's new tool, rebranded Codex, aims to assist and automate workflows, allowing for independent operation for extended periods. This advancement has the potential to significantly impact productivity. Codex can run for hours if needed, making it a valuable asset for various tasks.

content: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.
summary: A preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of using humanoid robots controlled by surgeons to perform operations, with a world-first operation being conducted on live pigs. This trial aims to assess the potential of humanoid robots in surgery. The use of robots in surgery could potentially revolutionize the field.

The case could have significant implications for AI companies and their handling of user data. OpenAI's actions may be seen as a misstep in its legal battle with news organizations.

title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
content: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.
summary: An Ivy League professor suspected AI cheating and ordered an in-person final, which resulted in scores falling by 50%. The professor believes AI cheating can lead to a failed society. This highlights the growing concern of AI-assisted cheating in academic settings.

title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
content: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.

This incident is part of a larger lawsuit against the company behind Grok, accused of enabling child predators. Multiple young girls are suing the company for its handling of child sexual abuse material.

title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
content: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."
summary: Hackers can utilize 9 popular AI tools to create massive botnets by exploiting the limitations of large language models, specifically their inability to respond with uncertainty. This vulnerability, known as HalluSquatting, allows attackers to assemble and control botnets. The discovery highlights potential security risks associated with AI tools.

title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
content: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.
summary: China's DeepSeek is planning to develop its own chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia and Huawei due to US export controls. This move is still in its early stages. The goal is to decrease reliance on foreign technology.

title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
content: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
summary: Top robotics researchers and founders discuss the evolution of robot autonomy, which could lead to autonomous robot workers in workplaces and homes. This development is made possible by advancements in artificial intelligence. The technology has the potential to significantly impact various industries and daily life.

title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision

summary: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts believes Bitcoin is nearing the late stages of its bear market and could reach $250,000 within the next couple of years. While he considers a $1 million target by 2030 premature, he expresses confidence in Bitcoin's significant near-term price appreciation.

title: Hyperliquid’s HIP-3 markets surge to nearly 50% of perp volume as onchain stock trading grows

This surge is attributed to the growth of onchain stock trading. The increase in HIP-3's share of perp volume indicates a notable shift in market activity.

China has largely prohibited crypto activities, but authorities remain vigilant in pursuing related crimes. This call for increased investigation highlights the ongoing challenges in regulating crypto in China.

title: Stablecoin FX priced below interbank rates in Q2, with routing now the biggest cost lever: Borderless

title: Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC
Strive (ASST) purchased 18 bitcoin for about $1.2 million last week, increasing its treasury to 19,900 BTC while maintaining $154.1 million in cash as it continues integrating its bitcoin-focused treasury strategy.
This post Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strive (ASST) has purchased 18 additional bitcoin for $1.2 million, bringing its total treasury to 19,900 BTC and $154.1 million in cash, as part of its bitcoin-focused strategy. This move highlights the company's ongoing commitment to bitcoin. The purchase is a notable addition to its existing treasury.

title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
content: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.
summary: Uber's Chief Product Officer discusses the company's ambitions, including its relationship with Waymo and the use of AI in its services. The company is working on its financial-services ambitions and has established an AV Labs data operation. AI is being integrated into Uber's services in ways that will impact riders and drivers.

title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
content: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.
summary: Nous Research, the company behind Hermes agent maker, is in talks for new funding with a valuation of $1.5 billion, aiming to raise at least $75 million led by Robot Ventures. The funding round also sees significant participation from USV and other prominent investors. This funding could potentially fuel further development in AI-powered agents.

title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
content: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
content: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.
summary: Waze has added new AI-powered features and customization updates, utilizing Google's Gemini AI assistant to enhance its capabilities and stay competitive with rival services like Apple Maps. This integration reflects Google's effort to expand Gemini's presence across its products. The updates aim to improve user experience on Waze.

title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
content: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."
summary: Meta has removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram after receiving backlash, stating that the feature missed its intended mark of providing a useful creative tool. The feature allowed public content to be referenced in certain ways, but users responded negatively. The company has since made the feature unavailable.

title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
content:
Siri AI in iOS 27.	

iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to the hype Apple promised in the keynote.
This year's iOS upgrades are what one might call a Snow Leopard update. That means it's light on new features and instead focused on fixing things that were broken and speeding up processes across the OS. App launches, Photos search results, and AirDrop transfers should all be faster. The Messages app now supports in-line replies and end-to-end encryption for RCS messages. Liquid Glass has gotten more refined, …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The first public beta of iOS 27 has been released, with a focus on fixing existing issues and improving performance, including enhancements to Siri AI. The update brings faster app launches and improved Photos search results. The Messages app also now supports in-line replies and end-to-end encryption for RCS messages.

title: Waze is getting a bunch of new AI-powered features
content:
Waze is getting an AI makeover. Google is integrating its flagship AI assistant, Gemini, into the driving app with the goal of letting users personalize their trips a little more. 
Of the four new updates, only two are being described as involving Gemini. Waze says its updating its conversation reporting feature, first introduced in 2024, to allow drivers to use conversational voice commands to report traffic incidents and suggest map updates, like a road closure or outdated house number. In addition, Waze introduced Destination Search, enabling drivers to use (again) use conversation voice commands (e.g., "Find me a coffee shop that's open …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Waze is integrating Google's AI assistant Gemini to introduce new features, including conversational voice commands for reporting traffic incidents and suggesting map updates, as well as a Destination Search feature. The updates aim to let users personalize their trips. The new features are part of Waze's AI makeover.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
content: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.
summary: The EU has warned Meta that it may face massive fines if it doesn't disable auto-play and infinite scroll on its platforms, as part of the Digital Services Act. This act may force Meta to make significant changes to its platforms. The regulation aims to improve user experience and reduce harm.

title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
content: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.
summary: Google has updated Android Bench with new large language models (LLMs), but Gemini still lags behind. This update allows developers to contribute to the evolution of Android Bench. The introduction of new LLMs aims to improve performance and efficiency.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: Advocates warn that Elon Musk's plan to stop monitoring content on X could pose a serious risk to the privacy of American users, especially given the platform's use of artificial intelligence for data analysis. They urge the FTC to reject the bid, emphasizing the need for stronger privacy protections.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
summary: The article provides a daily roundup of the latest news affecting the cryptocurrency market, covering topics such as Bitcoin price movements, blockchain developments, DeFi, Web3, and regulatory updates. It serves as a quick guide to current trends and events in the crypto space.

title: Strategy’s no-bitcoin-buy week shows ‘greater balance-sheet discipline,’ analysts say
content: Analysts from Benchmark and TD Cowen maintained Buy ratings and price targets of $570 and $260, respectively.
summary: Analysts are viewing a company's recent week without purchasing Bitcoin as an indication of greater balance-sheet discipline. Both Benchmark and TD Cowen analysts have maintained 'Buy' ratings and specific price targets for the company.

title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
content:
Lorde performing at the 2026 Governors Ball. | Photo: Siegfried Anthony/Billboard via Getty Images	

Lorde was performing at the Mad Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's likely she was taking a shot at festival sponsor Ray-Ban, which has collaborated with Meta on a pair of AI smart glasses.
The comments were captured in videos shared to social media. After thanking the crowd for being there and taking part in "something real," she said that it was increasingly hard to know what is and isn't real, before saying "You don't know if someone is wearing sunglasses or if they're wearing those fucked up fucking… Can I just say, …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: During a performance in Madrid, singer Lorde publicly criticized AI smartglasses, likely referring to the Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses, calling them "not sexy" and "fucked up." She expressed concerns about the difficulty of discerning reality with such devices, offering a celebrity's take on consumer AI technology.

title: U.S. inflation, second-quarter earnings reports: Crypto Week Ahead
content: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 13.
summary: This article briefly outlines the agenda for the 'Crypto Week Ahead' starting July 13. It highlights key economic factors such as U.S. inflation and second-quarter earnings reports as relevant topics for the crypto community.

title: CLARITY Act gains second law enforcement endorsement before Senate push
content: The Federal Law Enforcement Officers Association expressed support for the CLARITY Act but urged changes to the legislation’s language.
summary: The Federal Law Enforcement Officers Association has expressed support for the CLARITY Act, but suggested changes to the legislation's language, as the act prepares for a Senate push. The act's details are not provided in the article. The endorsement is the act's second from law enforcement.

Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic using sperm…

The story of Ties van der Meer, a 47-year-old conceived at a private fertility clinic, highlights the need for such limits. The article touches on AI world models but does not delve deeply into the topic.

content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US —Casey Crownhart I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year…

title: The Download: worms fight pollution, and geoengineering faces reality
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips to…
summary: A California dairy farmer is using worms and microbes to reduce manure pollution, a potential solution for environmental issues. This approach is part of a larger trend in using technology and nature to combat pollution. The method involves using worms to break down manure, reducing pollution and creating a more sustainable farming practice.

title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
content: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."
summary: Sam Altman responded to Elon Musk's accusations, calling out Musk for selling investors on short-term space data centers. The exchange is part of an ongoing feud between the two. Altman's statement reflects a common criticism among experts about the viability of space data centers.

title: Data centers’ energy demand threatens Trump’s “Made in America” plan
content: Squeeze on Rust Belt electricity bills threatens Trump’s manufacturing plan.
summary: The energy demand of data centers is threatening Donald Trump's Made in America plan by squeezing Rust Belt electricity bills, which could impact manufacturing. This could hinder Trump's goal of revitalizing American industry. Data centers' high energy consumption is a significant contributor to this issue.

title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
content: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might have identified those donors, he says. He…
summary: A European fertility group is calling for limits on sperm donors, citing concerns for individuals conceived through anonymous donation, like 47-year-old Ties van der Meer, who doesn't know how many siblings he has. The issue arose after a clinic in the Netherlands destroyed donor records. This affects people's ability to know their genetic lineage.

title: Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US
content: I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year the American holiday also marked a big symbolic deadline for US nuclear power. Last year the Trump administration set a goal to see three new microreactors achieve criticality, a technical milestone establishing that a reactor can…
summary: Four US nuclear reactors have achieved a significant milestone, reaching criticality, a technical milestone showing a reactor can sustain a nuclear chain reaction. This achievement was set as a goal by the Trump administration last year. The reactors are microreactors, a newer type of nuclear reactor.
